hypothesis: Adding a negative charge at the N‑terminus (position 1) and increasing hydrophobicity at position 2 will strengthen electrostatic and van der Waals interactions with the SSTR2 binding pocket, while keeping a small residue at position 6 will reduce steric clashes and improve overall binding affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The primary bottleneck is the overly stringent ddG threshold; relaxing this constraint while slightly increasing clash tolerance should improve candidate quality and pass rate.

proposed_changes.rationale: The ddG gate is rejecting many candidates that have highly negative ddG values, suggesting the threshold is too strict. Raising the threshold to -20.0 will allow more designs to pass while still filtering out overly destabilizing mutations.

Two candidates failed the clash gate, indicating that the current clash tolerance may be too tight for the generated structures. A modest increase to 15 will provide additional flexibility for side‑chain packing.
